---
title: Claim Grounding Test Manuscript
---

## Abstract

Our method achieves 90% accuracy, outperforming all state-of-the-art approaches on this benchmark dataset.

## Introduction

Neural networks have been shown to achieve 95% accuracy on standard image classification benchmarks.

Our approach is 3x faster than existing baseline methods in the literature.

Previous methods by Smith et al. [@smith2020] achieve only 80% accuracy on the same benchmark, which we exceed by 15%.

## Methods

We use standard gradient descent with a learning rate of 0.001 [@sgd2020].

The model architecture follows ResNet [@he2016deepresidual].

## Results

Compared to the baseline [@jones2021], our method improves accuracy by 15%.

## Discussion

The results suggest the method is superior to all compared approaches in the literature.

## References

- Smith, A., and Brown, B. (2020). Deep learning survey. Journal of ML Research.
- Jones, B. et al. (2021). Baseline method for classification. ICML.
- He, K. et al. (2016). Deep residual learning for image recognition. CVPR.
